page: Vultr vcg-a100-96c-896g-320vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an AMD x86_64 processor. The system features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, operating under a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 896.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.33 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3072 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile represents a high-density resource class designed for massive parallel processing. The combination of 320 GB of GPU VRAM, 896.0 GB of system memory, and NVMe storage provides a high-performance environment for workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, data science, and complex scientific simulations.
description: Vultr vcg-a100-96c-896g-320vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring an AMD x86_64 processor with 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs under a shared allocation. The system is equipped with 896.0 GB of system memory, yielding 9.33 GB per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 3072 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This high-density hardware configuration is designed for resource-intensive parallel computing tasks. It is suited for workloads such as large-scale machine learning, deep learning model training, data science pipelines, and complex scientific simulations requiring substantial GPU memory and high-speed local storage. The platform provides a balanced ratio of compute, memory, and high-speed storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
